item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is close, but it has just enough going for it that I can positively-rate it. It's clever enough, short enough, long enough, etc. Good, competent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You could mention up front that it supplies 500 mA at 9VDC indefinitely, which is what is implied, and then note that the voltage spikes high when the demon gets upset.

You can't actually spike the current output without also increasing the voltage anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, but I figured leaving it as voltage would make it easier for people reading to understand what is being said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but mA is a unit for current, not voltage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I definitely understand the feeling of missing something, it's why I sat on this article for so long. Perhaps in the future I'll figure out that extra spark (ha) the article needs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I f*cking love this one. I wish I could have learned what our trickster salesman sold at the beginning of the video log though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand the intent for a short punch here, implying things and getting in then getting out. For me, however, this is (1) a bit too subtle for the length and (2) missing something that I can't quite put my finger on. The content here is very…flippant, which is part of your point but it's one thing to attribute that flippancy to the characters and another to attach it to the tone of the article.

This is close, but I'm on the side of a -1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved the draft, loved the skip. +1

I still love your references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really how subtle you've made the "seller" in this iteration. There's still a clear hint who we're dealing with, but its not as in your face as it was before.

Overall really enjoyed the humorous take on Norse myth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pumped to see this finally up! Easy +1 for an actually original, clever, well-executed idea using mythological material - hardly a surprise, since it's you, but still gratifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those sneaky tricksters…

Thanks to everyone who helped critique and fine tune this idea! It was fun!

Photo is by me.
